input.headline: Paris court blocks auction of earliest-known calculator

raw.headline: Paris court blocks auction of earliest-known calculator

winner: Why did the ancient calculator get so upset in court? It said its rights were being *abaculated*!

candidates.text: Why did the ancient calculator get so upset in court? It said its rights were being *abaculated*!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative wordplay with 'abaculated' (a play on 'abacus' and 'ablated'), good specificity to the headline, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: This calculator was so old, even its buttons had a vintage look—turns out, they were more like "old" than "new.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'old' and 'new,' but it lacks specificity about the calculator's historical significance and feels a bit generic in its delivery."}

candidates.text: Paris court orders the latest known calculator to be put up for auction—apparently, it’s overqualified and too afraid to step into its predecessor’s shadow.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the twist of the calculator being 'overqualified,' but it lacks direct connection to the headline and feels a bit forced in its setup."}

candidates.text: The judge ruled that the abacus was too busy counting its own fingers.
The judge ruled that using a computer for calculations was simply too advanced for 19th-century technology.
